title: Early Warning Procedure (Works)
text_english: EARLY WARNING: The Contractor SHALL WARN the Engineer-in-Charge AT THE EARLIEST OPPORTUNITY of SPECIFIC LIKELY FUTURE EVENTS or CIRCUMSTANCES that MAY ADVERSELY AFFECT the Execution of Works. The Contractor SHALL COOPERATE with the Engineer-in-Charge in MAKING and CONSIDERING PROPOSALS for HOW the EFFECT of such an event/circumstance can be AVOIDED or REDUCED — and CARRY OUT any RESULTING INSTRUCTION. The Engineer-in-Charge MAY REQUIRE the Contractor to ATTEND a MANAGEMENT MEETING to (i) REVIEW the PROGRAMME for REMAINING WORK; AND (ii) DEAL with MATTERS RAISED in accordance with the EARLY WARNING PROCEDURE.

title: Authority's Representative — 100% Test Check on QC Tests
text_english: TESTS: For determining conformity to Specifications/Standards, the Authority's Representative SHALL REQUIRE the Contractor to CARRY OUT TESTS at TIMES, FREQUENCY and MANNER specified in Schedule-R per Good Industry Practice. The CHECKS by the Authority's Representative SHALL COMPRISE {{authority_check_pct}}% (default 100%) of all the QUANTITY or NUMBER of tests prescribed for each category/type of test for QUALITY CONTROL by the Contractor. In the event tests establish DEFECTS or DEFICIENCIES, the Contractor SHALL CARRY OUT REMEDIAL MEASURES at OWN COST and FURNISH a REPORT to the Authority's Representative.
